title: 10 best herbal tinctures for psoriasis

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe tinctures helps with psoriasis because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The gel inside the aloe vera plant contains powerful compounds that help to reduce inflammation, redness, and itching associated with psoriasis. The tincture is easily absorbed into the skin, providing quick relief from symptoms. Additionally, aloe's natural moisturizing properties help to hydrate dry, flaky skin, promoting healthy skin cell growth and reducing scaling. This natural remedy can be used topically or taken internally as a supplement, making it a gentle yet effective treatment option for psoriasis sufferers.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Aloe tincture's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with psoriasis, by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory molecules.

Wound-healing and tissue regeneration: Aloe tincture's ability to stimulate collagen synthesis, improve blood flow, and promote wound healing can help alleviate symptoms of psoriasis, such as skin thickening and scaling.

Antioxidant activity: Aloe tincture's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to psoriasis development and exacerbation, reducing oxidative stress and promoting overall skin health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the aloe plant contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, including saponins and anthraquinones, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in treating psoriasis.

Barks: Aloe barks are rich in flavonoids, terpenes, and other bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions such as psoriasis due to their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves from mature plants with thick, fleshy skin and no signs of disease or pests.
Wash the harvested aloe vera leaves thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and bacteria.
Chop the washed aloe vera leaves into small pieces using scissors or a knife to release their gel-like substance.
Soak the chopped aloe vera in 90% vodka or grain alcohol for 2 weeks, shaking the mixture daily, to extract its active compounds.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and transfer it to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids to store at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with psoriasis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The tannins and flavonoids present in the herb help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with psoriasis. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of pot marigold tincture can help to combat fungal and bacterial infections that may exacerbate psoriasis symptoms. As a result, using herbal pot marigold tinctures as part of an overall treatment plan can provide relief from psoriasis symptoms and promote healthier skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Pot marigold tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with psoriatic lesions.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant compounds in pot marigold tincture neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to psoriasis symptoms.
Immunomodulatory effects: Pot marigold's immunomodulatory properties may help regulate the immune system's response to psoriatic lesions, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in reducing inflammation and itching associated with psoriasis.
Seeds: Packed with antioxidants and fatty acids, which help soothe and calm irritated skin while promoting skin health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula officinalis flowers in their prime for maximum potency and quality.
Dry the harvested calendula officinalis flowers using a food dehydrator or air circulation method.
Combine 100g of dried calendula officinalis flowers with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily and checking for spoilage.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a dark glass bottle for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with psoriasis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compound curcumin in turmeric has been shown to reduce inflammation in the skin, which is a major contributor to psoriasis symptoms. By reducing inflammation, curcumin helps to alleviate redness, itching, and scaling associated with psoriasis. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help to protect the skin from further damage, promoting healthier skin cells and reduced appearance of psoriatic lesions.
remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: These bioactive compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the skin, thereby alleviating symptoms of psoriasis such as redness, swelling, and itching.

Cyclooxygenase (COX) inhibition: Turmeric's curcuminoids can inhibit COX enzymes, which are responsible for producing prostaglandins that promote inflammation in the body. By blocking COX activity, turmeric tinctures can help reduce psoriasis symptoms by decreasing inflammation and pain.

Modulation of cytokines: Curcumin in turmeric tinctures has been shown to modulate the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, such as TNF-alpha and IL-1β, which are involved in the pathogenesis of psoriasis. By regulating these cytokines, turmeric may help reduce inflammation and improve symptoms of psoriasis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Turmeric roots contain a high concentration of curcuminoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and soothe skin irritations associated with psoriasis.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of turmeric are the main source of curcumin, a potent bioactive compound that has been shown to reduce symptoms of psoriasis by inhibiting pro-inflammatory cytokines and improving skin barrier function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality turmeric roots in the morning when they are fresh and full of essential oils.
Dry the harvested turmeric roots using a food dehydrator on low heat for at least 24 hours.
Chop the dried turmeric roots into small pieces and add 4 cups of chopped root to 1 cup of vodka or 100 proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place, shaking the jar daily to ensure all parts are fully submerged.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and preserving the liquid for use within one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with psoriasis because its active compounds, such as aristolochic acid and flavonoids, possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These bioactive molecules can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with psoriatic skin lesions. Additionally, the tincture's soothing and calming effects may alleviate stress and anxiety, which are often linked to psoriasis exacerbation. By addressing these underlying factors, maidenhair tree tinctures can promote a more balanced skin environment, ultimately helping to manage and improve psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to psoriasis symptoms such as redness and swelling.
Immunomodulatory effects: May help to regulate the immune system's response to triggers, reducing excessive inflammation and skin cell proliferation that can occur in psoriasis.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Can help to reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with psoriasis by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes and chemical signals that perpetuate inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree contain a high concentration of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties that can help soothe and calm psoriatic symptoms.

Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree are rich in tannins, which have natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality ginkgo biloba leaves in late summer or early fall when they are fully ripe and dry them completely to prevent mold growth.
Chop the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into small pieces to increase their surface area for extraction.
Combine 1 part of the chopped ginkgo biloba leaves with 5 parts of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar to create a tincture ratio of 1:5.
Allow the mixture to macerate for at least four weeks, shaking the jar daily, before straining the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container.
Store the finished ginkgo biloba tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and label it clearly, allowing it to mature for at least six months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Siberian ginseng tinctures helps with psoriasis because it has been shown to reduce inflammation and improve overall health, which can help alleviate symptoms of the condition. The adaptogenic properties of Siberian ginseng allow it to help the body respond better to stress, which is often a trigger for psoriasis flares. Additionally, Siberian ginseng tinctures have been found to promote immune system balance and increase energy levels, both of which can be beneficial for individuals with psoriasis who may experience fatigue and weakness as a result of their condition.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Siberian Ginseng's adaptogenic properties help to reduce stress and promote resilience, which can alleviate symptoms of psoriasis by decreasing inflammation and improving overall immune function.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant compounds in Siberian Ginseng tinctures help to neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress, which can exacerbate psoriasis symptoms such as skin irritation and inflammation.

Immunomodulatory effects: Siberian Ginseng's immunomodulatory properties may help to regulate the immune system's response to psoriasis triggers, reducing the risk of chronic inflammation and promoting a more balanced immune response.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Siberian ginseng plant contain a rich source of bioactive compounds, including saponins and polyacetylenes, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm psoriasis symptoms.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Siberian ginseng possess a unique combination of flavonoids and phenolic acids that may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, making them effective in alleviating psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest eleutherococcus senticosus roots and dry them thoroughly in a warm and dark place for several weeks.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and combine them with high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the root-vodka mixture in a cool and dark place for 4 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure even extraction.
Strain the liquid from the jar using cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Bottle the resulting tincture in dark glass bottles and store it in a cool and dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort tinctures helps with psoriasis because it has natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe and calm irritated skin. The herb's active compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healthy skin cell growth, which can aid in the management of psoriasis symptoms like redness, scaling, and itching. By reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin cell turnover, St John's wort tinctures may help to improve overall skin health and alleviate psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: St. John's Wort tincture helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to psoriasis symptoms such as redness, swelling, and pain.
Immunomodulatory Effects: The herb has been shown to modulate the immune system, reducing excessive inflammation and preventing further tissue damage caused by chronic psoriasis.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: St. John's Wort tincture contains flavonoids and hypericin that have potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce swelling, redness, and itching associated with psoriasis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe skin irritation and reduce inflammation associated with psoriasis.

Leaves: Contain hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to exhibit potent anti-proliferative effects on keratinocytes, reducing cell proliferation and differentiation, which can help alleviate psoriatic symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves from a clean environment on a sunny day.
Cleanly sort and dry the plant material to ensure no moisture remains.
Crush 200 grams of dried st. john's wort into small pieces using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the crushed herb with 600 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a glass jar for two weeks shaking daily.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove solids and discard solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal tinctures helps with psoriasis because it contains berberine, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that reduces redness and swelling associated with the condition. Berberine also exhibits potent immunomodulatory effects, helping to regulate an overactive immune response that can contribute to psoriasis symptoms. Additionally, goldenseal's antiseptic properties help to prevent infection and promote wound healing, further alleviating symptoms of this chronic skin condition.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Has antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory effects that can help reduce inflammation and prevent infection in psoriatic lesions.
Berberein: Exhibits antioxidant and free radical-scavenging properties that can help mitigate oxidative stress and damage caused by chronic inflammation in psoriasis patients.

Hydrastine: Demonstrates anti-inflammatory and immune-modulating effects, which can help reduce the activity of inflammatory cells and prevent the activation of immune responses that exacerbate psoriasis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of goldenseal contain a high concentration of berberine, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in soothing skin irritations associated with psoriasis.

Rhyzomes: Goldenseal's rhyzomes are rich in alkaloids like berberine and hydropicrin, which help reduce inflammation and modulate the immune response, addressing underlying causes of psoriatic symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried goldenseal root weighing one pound for this recipe.
Clean the goldenseal root by rinsing it in cold running water then drying it.
Chop the cleaned goldenseal root into small pieces and combine it with 5 cups of vodka 95 percent in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks allowing the roots to infuse their properties into the vodka.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding the solids and store it in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm tinctures helps with psoriasis because its mucilages, rich in polysaccharides and flavonoids, create a soothing barrier on the skin, reducing inflammation and irritation. This natural emollient also helps to lock in moisture, calming dry, itchy skin often associated with psoriasis. Additionally, slippery elm's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce redness and swelling, allowing for improved skin flexibility and reduced appearance of psoriasis lesions. As a result, many people find relief from the discomfort and visual impact of psoriasis when using herbal slippery elm tinctures in conjunction with their existing treatment regimens.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Slippery elm tincture's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with psoriasis lesions, providing relief from itching and irritation.

Mucilages and emollients: The mucilage content in slippery elm tincture forms a protective barrier on the skin, locking in moisture and reducing inflammation, which helps to soothe and calm irritated skin affected by psoriasis.

Antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects: Slippery elm tincture's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, while its immunomodulatory effects can modulate the immune system response to reduce symptoms of psoriasis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilages, which help soothe and protect irritated skin, making it an effective treatment for psoriatic lesions.
Barks: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds like salicin, which can reduce inflammation and itching associated with psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh slippery elm bark from mature trees during the spring or fall seasons.
Dry the harvested bark in a warm and well-ventilated area for at least two weeks.
Grind 100g of dried slippery elm bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered slippery elm with 500ml of 60% ethanol vodka in a clean glass jar for four to six weeks.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and store it in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with psoriasis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe irritated skin. The tincture's glycyrrhizin content also reduces inflammation and slows down the progression of plaque buildup, common in psoriasis patients. Additionally, licorice root is known to have antioxidant effects, which help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthier skin cell growth and reducing redness and itching associated with psoriasis. This natural remedy can provide relief from psoriasis symptoms without harsh chemicals or medications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin, which can reduce inflammation in the skin and alleviate symptoms of psoriasis such as redness, swelling, and itching.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in licorice tincture, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate psoriasis symptoms like flaking and scaling.

Immunomodulatory effects: Licorice root has been shown to modulate the immune system, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress while promoting tissue repair, which can be beneficial in managing psoriasis, an autoimmune condition characterized by skin inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties and can help soothe skin irritations associated with psoriasis.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes contain a variety of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and alkaloids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and itching caused by psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality glycyrrhiza glabra roots in the morning after dew has dried and before the plant blooms.
Wash the harvested roots thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and debris.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces and combine them with 80 proof vodka at a ratio of 1 part root to 5 parts solvent.
Allow the mixture to steep for 6-8 weeks in a glass jar, shaking it daily, until the liquid has turned amber-colored.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into dark glass bottles and store it in a cool, dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with psoriasis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herb contains taraxasterol, a triterpenoid saponin that has been shown to reduce inflammation in the skin, which is a hallmark characteristic of psoriasis. Additionally, dandelion's high levels of vitamin A and beta-carotene help to promote healthy skin cell turnover, reducing the buildup of dead skin cells that can exacerbate psoriasis symptoms. By targeting both underlying inflammation and promoting healthy skin regeneration, dandelion tinctures may provide relief from psoriasis symptoms such as redness, itching, and scaling.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion contains sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with psoriasis.
Laxative properties: Dandelion's high levels of inulin and other fiber-rich compounds can help regulate digestion, reducing the likelihood of constipation, a common issue for people with psoriasis.

Antioxidant activity: Dandelion contains a range of antioxidants, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, both of which are thought to contribute to the development of psoriatic lesions.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dandelion plant have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions like psoriasis due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

Rhyzomes: Dandelion rhyzomes, which are underground stems that connect the roots to the leaves, contain inulin, a prebiotic fiber that can help soothe irritated skin and reduce inflammation associated with psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion roots taraxacum officinale in the morning after dew has dried collecting approximately 20-25 grams.
Wash the harvested roots gently with cold water remove dirt and debris and pat dry thoroughly.
Chop the cleaned dandelion roots into small pieces approximately 0.5 cm in length to increase surface area for solvent extraction.
Combine 20-25 grams of chopped dandelion roots with 100ml of 95% ethanol vodka or everclear in a clean glass jar ensuring all roots are submerged in liquid.
Allow the mixture to steep for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar daily and then filter the resulting tincture through cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle storing it in a cool dry place.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for psoriasis are liquid extracts made from plants, which have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of this chronic skin condition. These tinctures work by reducing inflammation and promoting healing, helping to soothe itchy, scaly skin and reduce redness and irritation. Examples of herbal tinctures that show promise in treating psoriasis include St. John's Wort, Chamomile, and Turmeric, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help calm the skin. By using these tinctures, individuals with psoriasis may experience improved quality of life, reduced discomfort, and enhanced overall well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat psoriasis with herbal tinctures is by combining Turmeric Tincture, rich in curcuminoids, with St. John's Wort Tincture, which has anti-inflammatory properties. Mix equal parts of both tinctures and take 20-30 drops three times a day. You can also add Ginger Tincture to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. Consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new treatment regimen.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to psoriasis that are treated with herbal tinctures are eczema, acne, and rosacea. Herbal remedies such as tea tree oil, aloe vera, and chamomile have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation. Some herbs like burdock root, yellow dock, and dandelion may also be used to address underlying digestive issues that can contribute to skin problems. These tinctures are often combined with other natural remedies to create a personalized treatment plan.
